TABULA TEMPORARIA — ANNO DOMINI 2026
============================================================
  Golden Number                XIII
  Dominical Letter             D
  Sundays after Epiphany       4
  Sundays after Pentecost      25

  MOVEABLE FEASTS
    Septuagesima Sunday      February 8
    Ash Wednesday            February 25
    Easter (Pascha) Day      April 12
    Ascension Day            May 21
    Pentecost                May 31
    Corpus Christi           June 11
    Advent Sunday            November 29

  EMBER DAYS
    Spring (Lent)            March 4, 6, 7
    Summer (Whitsun)         June 3, 5, 6
    Autumn (Holy Cross)      September 16, 18, 19
    Winter (Advent)          December 16, 18, 19


JANUARY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Thu     Circumcision of Our Lord                   [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. A great and wondrous mystery · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Great · No Suff.
  2  Fri  L  Octave Day of St Stephen                   [s]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. And Stephen, full of grace · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · Mag. Devout men buried Stephen · No Suff.
  3  Sat     Octave Day of St. John, Apostle & Evangelist [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This is the same John · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Holy Name of Jesus) · Mag. For he that is mighty · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of the Holy Innocents ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  4  Sun     Holy Name of Jesus                         [2cl] w
           Octave Day of the Holy Innocents (s)
           St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. He gave himself · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave Day of the Holy Innocents ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Titus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Thou shalt call · No Suff.
               Com. The Vigil of the Epiphany ("The Child Jesus")
               Com. St. Telesphorus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  5  Mon     The Vigil of the Epiphany                  [sd]  w
           St. Telesphorus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. While all things were in quiet silence · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Telesphorus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Epiphany of Our Lord) · Mag. The wise men · No Suff.
  6  Tue     EPIPHANY OF OUR LORD                       [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This day is the Church joined unto the Heavenly… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Now do we celebrate · No Suff.
  7  Wed     Day II within the Octave of the Epiphany   [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. From the east · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. When the wise men saw the star · No Suff.
  8  Thu     Day III within the Octave of the Epiphany  [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Three are the gifts · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Light of light · No Suff.
  9  Fri  L  Day IV within the Octave of the Epiphany   [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. We have seen his star · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Herod inquired · No Suff.
               Com. St. Paul the First Hermit ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 10  Sat     Day V within the Octave of the Epiphany    [sd]  w
           St. Paul the First Hermit (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Many nations · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Paul the First Hermit ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday within the Octave of Epiphany) · Mag. The child Jesus · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
 11  Sun     Sunday within the Octave of Epiphany       [sd]  w
           St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The child Jesus · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hyginus of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Son, why hast thou thus dealt with us? Behold… · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Epiphany ("When the wise men saw the star")
               Com. St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 12  Mon     Day VII within the Octave of the Epiphany  [sd]  w
           St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. They that despised thee · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Benedict Biscop, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Epiphany) · Mag. The wise men · No Suff.
 13  Tue     Octave Day of the Epiphany                 [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. This day is the Church joined unto the Heavenly… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Now do we celebrate · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 14  Wed     St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor     [d]   w
           St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Felix of Nola, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Maurus, Abbot) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. St. Hilary, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
 15  Thu     St Maurus, Abbot                           [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The world, and all things earthly · No Suff.
               Com. St. Marcellus I of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 16  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Marcellus I of Rome, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Marcellus I of Rome, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Anthony of Egypt) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
 17  Sat     St. Anthony of Egypt                       [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (II Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St. Anthony of Egypt ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. St Paul ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 18  Sun     II Sunday after Epiphany                   [sd]  g
           Chair of St Peter in Rome (gd)
           St Paul (com)
           St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. There was a marriage · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. St Paul ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Prisca of Rome, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. The wine failing · No Suff.
               Com. Chair of St Peter in Rome ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. St Paul ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 19  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs (com)
           St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Marius, Martha, Audifax, & Abachum, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Ephesus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs) · Mag. For theirs is the kingdom of heaven · No Suff.
 20  Tue     Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs   [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Agnes of Rome, Virgin & Martyr) · Mag. The blessed Agnes · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Fabian (Bishop) & Sebastian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 21  Wed     St. Agnes of Rome, Virgin & Martyr         [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Behold, now I see that · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. The blessed Agnes stood in the midst of the… · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vincent, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 22  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Ss Vincent, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Vincent, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. St. Emerentiana, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 23  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Emerentiana, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Emerentiana, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr) · Mag. This is a Martyr · No Suff.
 24  Sat     St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr                [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (III Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St Timothy, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Go forth, Ananias")
               Com. St. Peter ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 25  Sun     III Sunday after Epiphany                  [sd]  g
           Conversion of St. Paul (gd)
           St. Peter (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When Jesus · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Go forth, Ananias")
               Com. St. Peter ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. Lord, if Thou wilt · No Suff.
               Com. Conversion of St. Paul ("Go forth, Ananias")
               Com. St. Peter ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 26  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Polycarp of Smyrna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
 27  Tue     St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Cyril of Alexandria, Confessor, Bishop & Doctor) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. St. John Chrysostom, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 28  Wed     St Cyril of Alexandria, Confessor, Bishop & Doctor [d]   w
           The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. The Second Feast of St. Agnes, Virgin Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
 29  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. St. Martina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 30  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St. Martina, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Martina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
 31  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. A great and wondrous mystery · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (IV Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")

FEBRUARY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sun     IV Sunday after Epiphany                   [sd]  g
           St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr (d)
           St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. And when Jesus · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Bridget of Ireland, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Purification of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. The ancient · No Suff.
               Com. IV Sunday after Epiphany ("Lord, save us")
  2  Mon     Purification of the Blessed Virgin Mary    [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. And when the parents · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. To-day · No Suff.
  3  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Blaise, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Blaise, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Joseph of Aleppo, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. The New Martyrs of Russia ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  4  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Joseph of Aleppo, Martyr (com)
           The New Martyrs of Russia (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Joseph of Aleppo, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. The New Martyrs of Russia ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Agatha) · Mag. The blessed Agatha · No Suff.
  5  Thu     St. Agatha                                 [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The heathen multitude made haste · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Upon Him Who hath vouchsafed · No Suff.
               Com. St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Photius, Bishop and Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  6  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           St Photius, Bishop and Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Dorothy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Photius, Bishop and Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany) · Mag. God hath holpen · No Suff.
               Com. St Romuald, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  7  Sat     Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany      [sd]  g
           St Romuald, Abbot (d)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Sir · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Romuald, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Septuagesima Sunday) · Mag. The Lord said · Suff.
               Com. Office of the V Sunday after Epiphany ("The master saith: Gather ye together first the tares")
  8  Sun     Septuagesima Sunday                        [2cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The kingdom · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. The householder saith unto his labourers · Suff.
               Com. St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
  9  Mon     Feria                                            v
           St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Apollonia of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Scholastica, Virgin) · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
 10  Tue     St Scholastica, Virgin                     [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Septuagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Septuagesima ("And the householder said")
               Com. St Gregory II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 11  Wed     Feria                                            v
           St Gregory II, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Take that thine is · Suff.
 12  Thu     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Is it not lawful for me · Suff.
 13  Fri  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
               Com. St Valentine, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 14  Sat     Feria                                            v
           St Valentine, Priest & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Valentine, Priest & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Sexagesima Sunday) · Mag. The Lord · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 15  Sun     Sexagesima Sunday                          [2cl] v
           Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. When much people · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Faustinus & Jovita, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Jesus said to His disciples · Suff.
 16  Mon     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. If ye would be truly honourable · Suff.
 17  Tue     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The seed is the word of God · Suff.
               Com. St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 18  Wed     Feria                                            v
           St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Simeon of Jerusalem, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. But that on the good ground · Suff.
 19  Thu     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 20  Fri  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
 21  Sat     Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Quinquagesima Sunday) · Mag. Mighty Abraham · Suff.
 22  Sun     Quinquagesima Sunday                       [2cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Behold, we go up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Chair of St. Peter at Antioch) · Mag. Thou art the shepherd of the sheep · No Suff.
               Com. Quinquagesima Sunday ("The father of our faith")
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St Paul ("O holy Apostle Paul")
 23  Mon     Chair of St. Peter at Antioch              [2cl] w
           St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           St Paul (com)
           Vigil of St. Matthias (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Whatsoever · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Monday after Quinquagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
               Com. St Paul ("O holy Apostle Paul")
               Com. Vigil of St. Matthias ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. During his pontificate · No Suff.
               Com. St. Matthias, Apostle ("They will deliver you up")
               Com. St Peter Damian, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St Paul ("O holy Apostle Paul")
               Com. Monday after Quinquagesima ("And they that went before")
 24  Tue     St. Matthias, Apostle                      [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Quinquagesima ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be ye valiant in warfare · No Suff.
               Com. Tuesday after Quinquagesima ("Have mercy on me")
 25  Wed  L§ ASH WEDNESDAY                              [1cl] v
           St Walburga of Heidenheim, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. When ye fast · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Walburga of Heidenheim, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven · Suff.
               Com. St. Alexander of Alexandria, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 26  Thu  L§ Thursday after Ash Wednesday               [f2]  v
           St. Alexander of Alexandria, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Alexander of Alexandria, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O Lord · No Suff.
               Com. St. Raphael of Brooklyn ("Behold a great priest")
 27  Fri  L§ Friday after Ash Wednesday                 [f2]  v
           St. Raphael of Brooklyn (gd)
           Lauds   v · Ben. When thou dost · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Raphael of Brooklyn ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. But thou when thou shalt pray · No Suff.
               Com. St. Raphael of Brooklyn ("Behold a great priest")
 28  Sat  L§ Saturday after Ash Wednesday               [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. For they seek me · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (I Sunday in Lent) · Mag. Then shalt thou call · Suff.
               Com. St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")

MARCH
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sun  L  I SUNDAY IN LENT                           [1cl] v
           St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Then was Jesus · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St David of Wales, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Then shalt thou call · Suff.
               Com. St Chad, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  2  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent I                        [f2]  v
           St Chad, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Come, ye blessed of My Father · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Chad, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. As long as you did it to one · Suff.
  3  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent I                       [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus went into · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. For it is written · Suff.
               Com. St Lucius I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  4  Wed  L§ Lent Ember Wednesday                       [f2]  v
           St Lucius I, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. This evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Lucius I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. As Jonas was three days and three nights in… · Suff.
  5  Thu  L§ Thursday after Lent I                      [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And Jesus went from thence · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O woman · No Suff.
               Com. St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  6  Fri  L§ Lent Ember Friday                          [f2]  v
           St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Angel of the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He that made me whole · No Suff.
               Com. St Perpetua & Felicitas, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  7  Sat  L§ Lent Ember Saturday                        [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And Jesus taketh his disciples · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (II Sunday in Lent) · Mag. Tell the vision that ye have seen to no… · Suff.
  8  Sun  L  II SUNDAY IN LENT                          [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And Jesus taketh his disciples · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Tell the vision that ye have seen to no… · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
  9  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent II                       [f2]  v
           St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The beginning · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Nyssa, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He That sent Me is with Me · Suff.
               Com. The Forty Holy Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 10  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent II                      [f2]  v
           The Forty Holy Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. For One is your Master · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. The Forty Holy Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. And all ye are brethren · Suff.
 11  Wed  L§ Wednesday after Lent II                    [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Behold, we go up to Jerusalem · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Gregory the Great) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. Wednesday after Lent II ("For He shall be delivered to the Gentiles")
 12  Thu  L§ St. Gregory the Great                      [2cl] w
           Thursday after Lent II (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Gregory · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent II ("Son, remember")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent II ("That rich man")
 13  Fri  L§ Friday after Lent II                       [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. He will miserably destroy · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. When they sought · Suff.
 14  Sat  L§ Saturday after Lent II                     [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. I will arise and go to my father · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (III Sunday in Lent) · Mag. But the father said · Suff.
 15  Sun  L  III SUNDAY IN LENT                         [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. When a strong man armed · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. A certain woman of the company lifed up her… · Suff.
 16  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent III                      [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Amen I say unto you · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. But Jesus · No Suff.
               Com. St. Patrick ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Joseph of Arimathea, C ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 17  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent III                     [f2]  v
           St. Patrick (d)
           St Joseph of Arimathea, C (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. If two of you shall agree on earth · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Patrick ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Joseph of Arimathea, C ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Where two or three are gathered together in My… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Patrick ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St Edward, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 18  Wed  L§ Wednesday after Lent III                   [f2]  v
           St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           St Edward, King & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Hear and understand · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
               Com. St Edward, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. Joseph, being raised · No Suff.
               Com. Wednesday after Lent III ("To eat")
 19  Thu  L§ St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary [2cl] w
           Thursday after Lent III (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Jesus himself · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent III ("And devils also came out of many")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Behold a faithful · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Lent III ("All they that had")
               Com. St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 20  Fri  L§ Friday after Lent III                      [f2]  v
           St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Whosoever drinketh · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Cuthbert, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Benedict) · Mag. Let all the multitude · No Suff.
               Com. Friday after Lent III ("Sir, I perceive")
 21  Sat  L§ St. Benedict                               [2cl] w
           Saturday after Lent III (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Benedict, the man of God · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Saturday after Lent III ("Jesus stooped down")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Today holy Benedict · No Suff.
               Com. IV Sunday in Lent ("Woman, hath no man condemned thee?")
               Com. Saturday after Lent III ("My soul doth magnify the Lord, and my spirit…")
 22  Sun  L  IV SUNDAY IN LENT                          [1cl] p
           Lauds   p · Ben. When Jesus · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers p · II prec. · Mag. Woman, hath no man condemned thee? · Suff.
 23  Mon  L§ Monday after Lent IV                       [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Thus saith the Lord · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gabriel the Archangel ("The Angel Gabriel was sent")
 24  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Lent IV                      [f2]  v
           St. Gabriel the Archangel (gd)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Why go ye about to kill Me · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gabriel the Archangel ("The Angel Gabriel was sent")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Annunciation of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. The Holy Ghost · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gabriel the Archangel ("The Angel Gabriel was sent")
 25  Wed  L§ ANNUNCIATION OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY    [1cl] w
           Wednesday after Lent IV (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. How shall this be · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Wednesday after Lent IV ("Rabbi, who did sin, this man, or his parents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Angel Gabriel · No Suff.
 26  Thu  L§ Thursday after Lent IV                     [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus went · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. A great Prophet · No Suff.
               Com. St. John Damascene, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
 27  Fri  L§ Friday after Lent IV                       [f2]  v
           St. John Damascene, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Our friend Lazarus sleepeth · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. John Damascene, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Lord, if Thou hadst been here · No Suff.
               Com. St. John Damascene, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
 28  Sat  L§ Saturday after Lent IV                     [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Sunday of the Passion) · Mag. I am One · No Suff.
 29  Sun  L  SUNDAY OF THE PASSION                      [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Jesus said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Your father Abraham rejoiced to see My day · No Suff.
               Com. St John Climacus, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 30  Mon  L§ Monday after Passion Sunday                [f2]  v
           St John Climacus, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. In the last day, that great day of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St John Climacus, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. If any man thirst, let him come unto Me · No Suff.
               Com. St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 31  Tue  L§ Tuesday after Passion Sunday               [f2]  v
           St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. My time is not yet come · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Innocent of Alaska, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Go ye up unto this Feast · No Suff.

APRIL
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Wed  L§ Wednesday after Passion Sunday             [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. My sheep hear My voice · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Many good works have I wrought · No Suff.
  2  Thu  L§ Thursday after Passion Sunday              [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Master saith: My time is at hand · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. With desire I have desired · No Suff.
               Com. Our Lady of Sorrows ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
  3  Fri  L§ Friday after Passion Sunday                [f2]  v
           Our Lady of Sorrows (gd)
           Lauds   v · Ben. When Jesus saw His Mother · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Our Lady of Sorrows ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. When Jesus saw His Mother · No Suff.
               Com. Our Lady of Sorrows ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
  4  Sat  L§ Saturday after Passion Sunday              [f2]  v
           St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Father, glorify Thou Me · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Isidore of Seville, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (Palm Sunday) · Mag. It is written: I will smite the Shepherd · No Suff.
  5  Sun  L  PALM SUNDAY                                [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The multitudes · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. It is written: I will smite the Shepherd · No Suff.
  6  Mon  L§ HOLY MONDAY                                [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. And now, O Father, glorify Thou Me · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Thou couldest have no power · No Suff.
  7  Tue  L§ HOLY TUESDAY                               [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Now, before the Feast of the Passover, as Jesus… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. I have power · No Suff.
  8  Wed  L§ HOLY WEDNESDAY                             [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Simon, do you sleep? · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The handmaid said to Peter · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Palm Sunday ("My soul doth magnify the Lord, and my spirit…")
  9  Thu  L§ HOLY THURSDAY                              [1cl] w
           Thursday after Palm Sunday (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Now he that betrayed Him · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Thursday after Palm Sunday ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And, as they were eating · No Suff.
               Com. Good Friday ("God spared not His Own Son")
 10  Fri  L§ GOOD FRIDAY                                [1cl] b
           Friday after Palm Sunday (f2)
           Lauds   b · Ben. They set up over his head his accusation written · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Friday after Palm Sunday ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers b · II prec. · Mag. When He had received the vinegar · No Suff.
               Com. Holy Saturday ("O all ye that pass by")
 11  Sat  L§ HOLY SATURDAY                              [1cl] w
           St Leo the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Saturday after Palm Sunday (f2)
           Lauds   w · Ben. There were women sitting over against the sepulchre · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Leo the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
               Com. Saturday after Palm Sunday ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday of the Resurrection) · Mag. And looking · No Suff.
 12  Sun     SUNDAY OF THE RESURRECTION                 [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. And very early in the morning · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And looking · No Suff.
 13  Mon     EASTER MONDAY                              [1cl] w
           St Hermengild, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. While they walked · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermengild, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. What manner of communications are these · No Suff.
 14  Tue     EASTER TUESDAY                             [1cl] w
           St Justin, Martyr (com)
           St. Tiburtius, Valerian & Maximus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Jesus stood in the midst of His disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Justin, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St. Tiburtius, Valerian & Maximus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Behold My Hands · No Suff.
 15  Wed     Day IV within the Octave of Easter         [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Cast the net on the right side of the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Jesus saith · No Suff.
 16  Thu     Day V within the Octave of Easter          [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Mary stood without at the Sepulchre weeping · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. They have taken away my Lord · No Suff.
 17  Fri     Day VI within the Octave of Easter         [sd]  w
           St Anicetus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. When the eleven disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Anicetus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All power is given unto Me · No Suff.
 18  Sat     Day VII within the Octave of Easter        [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. They ran both together · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Low Sunday (Octave of Easter)) · Mag. The same day · No Suff.

           Lauds   w · Ben. The same day · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Tikhon of Moscow) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
 20  Mon     ST. TIKHON OF MOSCOW                       [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Lord loved him · No Suff.
 21  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I go before you into Galilee · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Because thou hast seen me · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Soter & Caius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 22  Wed     Feria                                            w
           Ss. Soter & Caius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am the True Vine · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Soter & Caius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. George) · Mag. Light perpetual · No Suff.

           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces

           Hours   No Preces

           Hours   No Preces

               Com. II Sunday after Easter ("I am the Shepherd of the Sheep")

           Day IV within the Octave of St. George (sd)

           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Day V within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")

           Hours   No Preces

           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Octave Day of St. George ("Light perpetual")

           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Octave Day of St. George ("Whosoever shall confess Me")

MAY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Fri  L  Ss. Philip and James, Apostles             [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the way · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If ye abide in me · No Suff.
               Com. St. Athanasius ("O Teacher right excellent")
  2  Sat     St. Athanasius                             [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Finding of the Holy Cross) · Mag. O Cross · No Suff.
               Com. St. Athanasius ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("Amen, amen I say to you")
  3  Sun     Finding of the Holy Cross                  [2cl] r
           Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- (com)
           III Sunday after Easter (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thou alone · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Alexander (Bp), Eventius, Theodulus (Mm) & Juve- ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("A little while")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. He the holy Cross endured · No Suff.
               Com. III Sunday after Easter ("Amen, amen I say to you")
  4  Mon     Feria                                            w
           St. Monica, Widow (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Monica, Widow ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · No Suff.
  5  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. John before the Latin Gate) · Mag. The blessed Apostle John · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  6  Wed     St. John before the Latin Gate             [gd]  r
           Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Daughters of Jerusalem · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of the Solemnity of St Joseph ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. O ye holy and righteous · No Suff.
               Com. St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  7  Thu     Feria                                            w
           St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And He went in with them · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Alexis Toth, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Apparition of St. Michael the Archangel) · Mag. While John beheld · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  8  Fri  L  Apparition of St. Michael the Archangel    [gd]  w
           St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. There was silence · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface IV, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Prince most glorious · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
  9  Sat     St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (IV Sunday after Easter) · Mag. Now I go my way · No Suff.
               Com. St. Gregory Nazianzen, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 10  Sun     IV Sunday after Easter                     [sd]  w
           Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Now I go my way · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Gordian & Epimachus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Now I go my way · No Suff.
 11  Mon     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Did not our heart burn within us, at the… · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I tell you the truth · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 12  Tue     Feria                                            w
           Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Peace be unto you · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nereus, Achilles, Domitilla & Pancras, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. When the Comforter, the Spirit of truth, is come · No Suff.
 13  Wed     Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. A spirit hath not flesh and bones · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I have yet many things to say unto you… · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 14  Thu     Feria                                            w
           St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The disciples gave the Lord a piece · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface of Tarsus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. For He shall not speak of Himself · No Suff.
 15  Fri  L  Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. These are the words · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
 16  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. O Queen of Heaven, be joyful, alleluia · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (V Sunday after Easter) · Mag. Hitherto · No Suff.
 17  Sun     V Sunday after Easter                      [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Hitherto · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Ask, and ye shall receive · No Suff.
               Com. St Venantius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 18  Mon     Rogation Monday                            [f2]  v
           St Venantius, Martyr (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Ask, and ye shall receive · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Venantius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The Father Himself loveth you · No Suff.
               Com. St Venantius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Pudentiana, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 19  Tue     Feria                                            w
           St Pudentiana, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Christ ought to have suffered · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Pudentiana, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. I came forth from the Father · No Suff.
 20  Wed     Vigil of the Ascension                     [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Father, the hour is come · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Ascension of Our Lord) · Mag. Father · No Suff.
 21  Thu     ASCENSION OF OUR LORD                      [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O King of glory · No Suff.
 22  Fri  L  Day II within the Octave of the Ascension  [sd]  w
           St. Romanus, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Romanus, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O King of glory · No Suff.
 23  Sat     Day III within the Octave of the Ascension [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday within the Octave of the Ascension) · Mag. When the Comforter is come · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 24  Sun     Sunday within the Octave of the Ascension  [sd]  w
           St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. When the Comforter is come · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Vincent of Lerins, Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. These things have I told · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Urban, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 25  Mon     Day V within the Octave of the Ascension   [sd]  w
           St Urban, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Urban, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
               Com. St Eleutherius, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
 26  Tue     St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor [d]   w
           St Eleutherius, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Eleutherius, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. St Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St John I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
 27  Wed     St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor  [d]   w
           St John I, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St John I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of the Ascension ("Ye men of Galilee")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Ascension) · Mag. Father · No Suff.
               Com. St Bede the Venerable, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
 28  Thu     Octave Day of the Ascension                [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I ascend · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O King of glory · No Suff.
 29  Fri  L  Feria                                            w
           Lauds   w · Ben. But when the Comforter is come · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · No Suff.
               Com. St Felix I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 30  Sat  L§ VIGIL OF PENTECOST                         [1cl] w
           St Felix I, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Felix I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Whitsunday — Pentecost) · Mag. I will not leave you · No Suff.
               Com. St Petronilla, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 31  Sun     WHITSUNDAY — PENTECOST                     [1cl] r
           St Petronilla, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Receive ye · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Petronilla, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. This day · No Suff.

JUNE
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Mon     DAY II WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST      [1cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. God so loved the world · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. If a Man love Me · No Suff.
  2  Tue     DAY III WITHIN THE OCTAVE OF PENTECOST     [1cl] r
           Ss. Marcellinus, Peter & Erasmus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the Door · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Marcellinus, Peter & Erasmus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Peace I leave with you · No Suff.
  3  Wed  L§ Day IV within the Octave of Pentecost      [sd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. I am the Living Bread · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. I am the Living Bread Which came down from… · No Suff.
  4  Thu     Day V within the Octave of Pentecost       [sd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Jesus called His twelve disciples · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. The Comforter · No Suff.
  5  Fri  L§ Day VI within the Octave of Pentecost      [sd]  r
           St Boniface, Bishop & Martyr (d)
           Lauds   r · Ben. But that ye may know · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Boniface, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. But the Comforter which is the Holy Ghost · No Suff.
  6  Sat  L§ Day VII within the Octave of Pentecost     [sd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The charity of God · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Trinity Sunday) · Mag. Thanks, O God · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("My soul doth magnify the Lord, and my spirit…")
  7  Sun     TRINITY SUNDAY                             [1cl] w
           I Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed be · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. With all our heart · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday after Pentecost ("My soul doth magnify the Lord, and my spirit…")
  8  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Columba of Iona, Abbot) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  9  Tue     St Columba of Iona, Abbot                  [d]   w
           Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Primus & Felician, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The world, and all things earthly · No Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 10  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Scotland, Queen & Widow ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Corpus Christi) · Mag. O how sweet · No Suff.
               Com. St. Barnabas, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
 11  Thu     CORPUS CHRISTI                             [1cl] w
           St. Barnabas, Apostle (gd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Barnabas, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O sacred banquet · No Suff.
 12  Fri  L  Day II within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Ss Basilides, Cyrinus, Nabor & Nazarius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Basilides, Cyrinus, Nabor & Nazarius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how sweet · No Suff.
 13  Sat     Day III within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Sunday within the Octave of Corpus Christi) · Mag. The child Samuel · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Corpus Christi ("Christ the Lord, being made an High Priest for…")
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. All Saints of Antioch ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 14  Sun     Sunday within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           II Sunday after Pentecost
           St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor (gd)
           All Saints of Antioch (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. A certain man · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
               Com. All Saints of Antioch ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Go out quickly into the streets · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Corpus Christi ("Christ the Lord, being made an High Priest for…")
               Com. St Basil the Great, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 15  Mon     Day V within the Octave of Corpus Christi  [sd]  w
           Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vitus, Modestus & Crescentia, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O sacred banquet · No Suff.
 16  Tue     Day VI within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how sweet · No Suff.
 17  Wed     Day VII within the Octave of Corpus Christi [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of Corpus Christi) · Mag. O sacred banquet · No Suff.
               Com. St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 18  Thu     Octave Day of Corpus Christi               [gd]  w
           St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor (d)
           Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. I am · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Ephrem the Syrian, Deacon, Confessor & Doctor ("Well done")
               Com. Ss Mark & Marcellianus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces

 19  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss. Gervase & Protase, Martyrs (com)

               Com. Ss. Gervase & Protase, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces

               Com. St Silverius, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces

           Lauds   g · Ben. What man of you · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. What woman · No Suff.
               Com. St Alban, Protomartyr of England ("This is a Martyr")
               Com. St Paulinus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")

           Hours   Preces

           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The child · No Suff.

           Lauds   w · Ben. The mouth of Zacharias · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")

           Octave of the Compassion of Our Lord Jesus Christ (com)

           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
 27  Sat     Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist [sd]  w
           Vigil of Ss Peter & Paul (s)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The mouth of Zacharias · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of Ss Peter & Paul ("Blessed be the Lord God of Israel, for he…")
           Hours   No Preces

               Com. Day IV within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
               Com. St Leo II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
 28  Sun     IV Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g

           Lauds   g · Ben. And Jesus entered · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Leo II, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Ss. Peter and Paul, Apostles) · Mag. Thou art the shepherd of the sheep · No Suff.
 29  Mon     SS. PETER AND PAUL, APOSTLES               [1cl] r
           Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Whatsoever · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Today · No Suff.
               Com. St Paul, Apostle ("O Holy Apostle Paul")
 30  Tue     Commemoration of St Paul, Apostle          [gd]  w
           St Peter, Apostle (com)
           Day VII within the Octave of St John Baptist (sd)
           Day II within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Peter, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
