               Com. St Paul, Apostle ("O holy Apostle Paul")
               Com. St Peter, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")

JULY
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Wed     Octave of St John Baptist                  [gd]  w
           Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The mouth of Zacharias · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. Blessed art thou · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of St John Baptist ("He shall go before him")
               Com. Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  2  Thu     Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary      [2cl] w
           Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs (com)
           St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Day IV within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. When Elisabeth · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Processus & Martinian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St John of San Francisco, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. Day IV within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations · No Suff.
               Com. St Irenaeus of Lyons, Bishop & Martyr ("This is a Martyr")
  3  Fri  L  St Irenaeus of Lyons, Bishop & Martyr      [d]   r
           Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Whosoever will come after me · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. Day V within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  4  Sat     Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul [sd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Whatsoever · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (V Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Ye mountains · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  5  Sun     V Sunday after Pentecost                   [sd]  g
           Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul (sd)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Ye have heard that it was said · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. If thou bring thy gift to the Altar · No Suff.
               Com. The Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Thou art the shepherd of the sheep")
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of Ss Peter & Paul ("Peter and John")
  6  Mon     The Octave of Ss Peter & Paul              [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Whatsoever · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Today · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyril and Methodius, Bishops & Confessors ("How beautiful")
  7  Tue     Ss. Cyril and Methodius, Bishops & Confessors [d]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. In holiness · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. These are · No Suff.
  8  Wed     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
  9  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. The Seven Holy Brothers, with Ss Rufina & Secunda, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Joseph of Damascus (Priest) & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 10  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           The Seven Holy Brothers, with Ss Rufina & Secunda, Martyrs (com)
           St. Joseph of Damascus (Priest) & companions, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. The Seven Holy Brothers, with Ss Rufina & Secunda, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Joseph of Damascus (Priest) & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
               Com. St Pius I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 11  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           St Pius I, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Pius I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (VI Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. O Lord, I beseech thee · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 12  Sun     VI Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The multitude being very great · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nabor & Felix, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. I have compassion on the multitude · Suff.
               Com. St Anacletus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 13  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Anacletus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Anacletus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
 14  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. St Henry, Emperor & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 15  Wed     St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor      [d]   w
           St Henry, Emperor & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Henry, Emperor & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Our Lady of Einsiedeln) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Vladimir of Kiev, King & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 16  Thu     Our Lady of Einsiedeln                     [gd]  w
           Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Nicholas & Habib Khasha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Alexius, Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 17  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Alexius, Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Alexius, Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 18  Sat     Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn      [d]   w
           St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot (com)
           St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Sergius of Radonezh, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Symphorosa & her seven sons, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (VII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Zadok the priest · No Suff.
               Com. Translation of St Raphael of Brooklyn ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 19  Sun     VII Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Beware · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Seraphim of Sarov, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit · No Suff.
               Com. St Elias the Prophet ("I will liken him unto a wise man")
               Com. St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 20  Mon     St Elias the Prophet                       [d]   w
           St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Margaret of Antioch, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The world, and all things earthly · No Suff.
               Com. St Praxedes, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 21  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Praxedes, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Praxedes, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Mary Magdalene) · Mag. A woman in the city · No Suff.
 22  Wed     St. Mary Magdalene                         [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Mary anointed · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. A woman · No Suff.
               Com. St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St John Cassian, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 23  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           St John Cassian, Abbot (com)
           St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Apollinaris of Ravenna, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St John Cassian, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Liborius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. St Christina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 24  Fri  L  Vigil of St. James                         [s]   v
           St Christina, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Christina, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. James the Greater, Apostle) · Mag. They will deliver you up · No Suff.
               Com. St Christopher, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 25  Sat     St. James the Greater, Apostle             [2cl] r
           St Christopher, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Christopher, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be ye valiant in warfare · No Suff.
               Com. St. Anne, Mother of the Blessed Virgin Mary ("The kingdom of heaven")
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("What shall I do?")
 26  Sun     St. Anne, Mother of the Blessed Virgin Mary [2cl] w
           VIII Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Give her of the fruit of her hands · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("The lord said")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. She stretcheth out · No Suff.
               Com. VIII Sunday after Pentecost ("What shall I do?")
 27  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Pantaleon, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Pantaleon, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nazarius, Celsus, Martyrs; St Victor, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Ss. Nazarius, Celsus, Martyrs; St Victor, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Nazarius, Celsus, Martyrs; St Victor, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Martha of Bethany, Virgin) · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs ("Behold a great priest")
 29  Wed     St. Martha of Bethany, Virgin              [d]   w
           Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix II (Bishop), Simplicius, Faustinus & Beatrice, Martyrs ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 30  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Abdon & Sennen, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 31  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Chains of St Peter, Apostle) · Mag. Thou art the shepherd of the sheep · No Suff.
               Com. St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Holy Maccabees, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")

AUGUST
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sat     Chains of St Peter, Apostle                [gd]  w
           St Paul, Apostle (com)
           Holy Maccabees, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Thus saith the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. Holy Maccabees, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (IX Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Wisdom hath builded her house · No Suff.
               Com. Chains of St Peter, Apostle ("Thus saith the Lord")
               Com. St Paul, Apostle ("This is my commandment")
               Com. St Stephen, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  2  Sun     IX Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           St Stephen, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When the Lord was come near to Jerusalem · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. It is written: My house is the house of… · No Suff.
               Com. The Finding of St. Stephen, Protomartyr ("This is a Martyr")
  3  Mon     The Finding of St. Stephen, Protomartyr    [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. And Stephen · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Devout men · No Suff.
  4  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows) · Mag. O holy Mary · No Suff.
               Com. St Oswald, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  5  Wed     Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows        [gd]  w
           St Oswald, King & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Oswald, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Transfiguration of Our Lord) · Mag. Christ Jesus · No Suff.
               Com. Dedication of Our Lady of the Snows ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
               Com. Ss. Sixtus II (Bishop & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  6  Thu     Transfiguration of Our Lord                [2cl] w
           Ss. Sixtus II (Bishop & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. And behold a voice · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sixtus II (Bishop & Martyr), Felicissimus & Agapitus ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And when the disciples heard it · No Suff.
  7  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           St Donatus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Donatus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyriacus, Largus & Smaragdus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  8  Sat     Vigil of St. Lawrence, Martyr              [s]   v
           Ss. Cyriacus, Largus & Smaragdus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyriacus, Largus & Smaragdus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (X Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. I dwell · Suff.
               Com. St Romanus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  9  Sun     X Sunday after Pentecost                   [sd]  g
           St Romanus, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. And the publican · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Romanus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Lawrence, Martyr) · Mag. Laurence the Deacon · No Suff.
               Com. X Sunday after Pentecost ("This man went down to his house")
 10  Mon     St. Lawrence, Martyr                       [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. On the iron grate · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Blessed Laurence · No Suff.
 11  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Ss Tiburtius & Susanna, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Tiburtius & Susanna, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
 12  Wed     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Maximus of Constantinople, Confessor) · Mag. I will liken him unto a wise man · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 13  Thu     St Maximus of Constantinople, Confessor    [d]   w
           Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done, good and faithful servant · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Hippolytus & Cassian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The world, and all things earthly · No Suff.
               Com. St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 14  Fri  L§ Vigil of the Assumption                    [s]   v
           St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Eusebius, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. O most prudent Virgin · No Suff.
 15  Sat     ASSUMPTION OF THE BLESSED VIRGIN MARY      [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
               Com. St Joachim, Father of the B.V.M ("Let us praise a man famous")
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("He hath done all things well")
 16  Sun     St Joachim, Father of the B.V.M            [2cl] w
           XI Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Day II within the Octave of the Assumption (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Let us praise a man famous · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("When the Lord had passed")
               Com. Day II within the Octave of the Assumption ("Through thee have the gates of Paradise been thrown…")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The world, and all things earthly · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Octave of the Assumption ("Through thee have the gates of Paradise been thrown…")
               Com. XI Sunday after Pentecost ("He hath done all things well")
 17  Mon     Day III within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           Octave of St Laurence (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Octave of St Laurence ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
               Com. St Agapitus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Helen, Empress ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 18  Tue     Day IV within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           St Agapitus, Martyr (com)
           St Helen, Empress (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Agapitus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Helen, Empress ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
 19  Wed     Day V within the Octave of the Assumption  [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
 20  Thu     Day VI within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
 21  Fri  L  Day VII within the Octave of the Assumption [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave Day of the Assumption) · Mag. O most prudent Virgin · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm ("Behold a great priest")
 22  Sat     Octave Day of the Assumption               [gd]  w
           Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm (com)
           Vigil of St. Bartholomew (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Sing to the Lord · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Timothy (Bishop), Hippolytus & Symphorian, Mm ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. Vigil of St. Bartholomew ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. On this day · No Suff.
               Com. XII Sunday after Pentecost ("A certain man went down from Jerusalem")
 23  Sun     XII Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Master · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Bartholomew, Apostle) · Mag. They will deliver you up · No Suff.
               Com. XII Sunday after Pentecost ("A certain man went down from Jerusalem")
 24  Mon     St. Bartholomew, Apostle                   [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be ye valiant in warfare · No Suff.
 25  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Zephyrinus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 26  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Zephyrinus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Zephyrinus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
 27  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Fri  L  St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [gd]  w
           St Hermes, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hermes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (Beheading of St. John the Baptist) · Mag. Herod sent · No Suff.
               Com. St Augustine of Hippo, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St Sabina, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 29  Sat     Beheading of St. John the Baptist          [gd]  r
           St Sabina, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Sabina, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XIII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Now when Job had heard · No Suff.
               Com. Beheading of St. John the Baptist ("For Herod had laid hold upon John")
               Com. Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 30  Sun     XIII Sunday after Pentecost                [sd]  g
           Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. As Jesus passed through · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Felix & Adauctus, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. And one of them · Suff.
               Com. St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 31  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Aidan of Lindisfarne, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St Giles, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")

SEPTEMBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Giles, Abbot (com)
           Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Giles, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Twelve Holy Brothers, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  2  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Stephen of Hungary, King & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
  3  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Gorazde of Prague, Bishop & Martyr) · Mag. This is a Martyr · No Suff.
  4  Fri  L  St Gorazde of Prague, Bishop & Martyr      [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Whosoever will come after me · No Suff.
  5  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XIV Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. In all this · Suff.
  6  Sun     XIV Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Be not therefore anxious · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. Seek ye first the kingdom of God · Suff.
  7  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
               Com. St Hadrian, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  8  Tue     Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary        [2cl] w
           St Hadrian, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. To-day let us celebrate · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Hadrian, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Thy Nativity · No Suff.
  9  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Gorgonius, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gorgonius, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
 10  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. Ss Protus & Hyacinth, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 11  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss Protus & Hyacinth, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss Protus & Hyacinth, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (The Most Holy Name of Mary) · Mag. O holy Mary · No Suff.
 12  Sat     The Most Holy Name of Mary                 [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XV Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Remember not · No Suff.
               Com. The Most Holy Name of Mary ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 13  Sun     XV Sunday after Pentecost                  [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Jesus went into a city · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. A great prophet is risen up among us · No Suff.
               Com. Exaltation of the Holy Cross ("O Cross")
 14  Mon     Exaltation of the Holy Cross               [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Thou alone · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Seven Sorrows of the B.V.M) · Mag. Look not · No Suff.
               Com. Exaltation of the Holy Cross ("Thou alone")
               Com. St Nicomedes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 15  Tue     Seven Sorrows of the B.V.M                 [2cl] w
           St Nicomedes, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Come ye · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Nicomedes, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Sorrow oppresseth me · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cornelius (Bishop) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 16  Wed  L§ September Ember Wednesday                  [f2]  v
           Ss. Cornelius (Bishop) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs (d)
           Ss Euphemia, Lucy & Geminian, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cornelius (Bishop) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Ss Euphemia, Lucy & Geminian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cornelius (Bishop) & Cyprian (Bishop), Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 17  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 18  Fri  L§ September Ember Friday                     [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · No Suff.
               Com. St Januarius & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 19  Sat  L§ September Ember Saturday                   [f2]  v
           St Januarius & companions, Martyrs (d)
           St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Januarius & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St Theodore of Canterbury, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVI Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Adonai · No Suff.
               Com. St Eustace & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 20  Sun     XVI Sunday after Pentecost                 [sd]  g
           St Eustace & companions, Martyrs (d)
           Lauds   g · Ben. As Jesus went · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Eustace & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist) · Mag. They will deliver you up · No Suff.
               Com. XVI Sunday after Pentecost ("When thou art bidden")
 21  Mon     St. Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist        [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Ye which have forsaken all · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Be ye valiant in warfare · No Suff.
 22  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Maurice & companions, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Maurice & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Let my spirit rejoice · Suff.
               Com. St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St. Linus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 23  Wed     Feria                                            g
           St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           St. Linus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. From the hand of all that hate us · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Thecla, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St. Linus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. The Lord hath regarded · Suff.
 24  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 25  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 26  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cyprian & Justina, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. O Lord · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 27  Sun     XVII Sunday after Pentecost                [sd]  g
           Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Master · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Cosmas & Damian, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. What think ye of Christ? Whose Son is He? · Suff.
               Com. St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 28  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Wenceslaus, Duke, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Dedication of St. Michael the Archangel) · Mag. While John beheld · No Suff.
 29  Tue     DEDICATION OF ST. MICHAEL THE ARCHANGEL    [1cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. There was silence in heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Prince most glorious · No Suff.
               Com. St Jerome, Priest, Confessor & Doctor ("O Teacher right excellent")
 30  Wed     St Jerome, Priest, Confessor & Doctor      [gd]  w
           St Gregory of Armenia, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Gregory of Armenia, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")

OCTOBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Remigius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Holy Guardian Angels) · Mag. Are they not all · No Suff.
  2  Fri  L  Holy Guardian Angels                       [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Angel that talked · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Praise ye God · No Suff.
  3  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XVIII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. The Lord open your hearts · Suff.
  4  Sun     XVIII Sunday after Pentecost               [sd]  g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord said · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. And immediately, he that had been sick of the… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Placidus & companions, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  5  Mon     St. Placidus & companions, Martyrs         [gd]  r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The very hairs of your head · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. In the heavenly kingdom · No Suff.
  6  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (The Holy Rosary of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  7  Wed     The Holy Rosary of the Blessed Virgin Mary [gd]  w
           Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs (com)
           St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Sergius, Bacchus, Marcellus & Apuleius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. St. Mark of Rome, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
  8  Thu     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Denys, Rusticus & Eleutherius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  9  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Ss. Denys, Rusticus & Eleutherius, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Denys, Rusticus & Eleutherius, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.
 10  Sat     Saturday Office of the B.V.M               [s]   w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Motherhood of the B.V.M) · Mag. With peaceful joy · No Suff.
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("And the King came in")
 11  Sun     Motherhood of the B.V.M                    [2cl] w
           XIX Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. O holy Mary · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("Tell them which are bidden")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Thy Motherhood · No Suff.
               Com. XIX Sunday after Pentecost ("And the King came in")
 12  Mon     Feria                                            g
           St Wilfrid, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Wilfrid, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St. Edward, King & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
 13  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St. Edward, King & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St. Edward, King & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Callistus, Bishop & Martyr) · Mag. This is a Martyr · No Suff.
 14  Wed     St. Callistus, Bishop & Martyr             [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Our Lady of Walsingham) · Mag. O holy Mary · No Suff.
               Com. St. Callistus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")

           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Saturday Office of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · Suff.

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Luke, Evangelist) · Mag. They will deliver you up · No Suff.
               Com. XX Sunday after Pentecost ("So the father knew")
 18  Sun     St. Luke, Evangelist                       [2cl] r
           XX Sunday after Pentecost (sd)

               Com. XX Sunday after Pentecost ("There was a certain nobleman")
           Hours   No Preces

               Com. XX Sunday after Pentecost ("So the father knew")

           Hours   Preces

           Hours   Preces

           Hours   Preces

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Raphael the Archangel) · Mag. I am the Angel Raphael · No Suff.
 24  Sat     St Raphael the Archangel                   [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Be of good courage, Tobias · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (The Feast of O..J.C. The King) · Mag. The Lord God shall give unto him · No Suff.
               Com. St Raphael the Archangel ("I am the Angel Raphael")
               Com. Ss Chrysanthus & Daria, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. XXI Sunday after Pentecost ("O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all")
 25  Sun     THE FEAST OF O..J.C. THE KING              [1cl] w
           Ss Chrysanthus & Daria, Martyrs (com)

           Lauds   w · Ben. Unto God and his Father · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Chrysanthus & Daria, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. XXI Sunday after Pentecost ("Then said the lord")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. And he hath on his garment · No Suff.
               Com. XXI Sunday after Pentecost ("O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all")

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.

           Hours   Preces

           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 30  Fri  L§ Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
 31  Sat  L§ Vigil of All Saints                        [s]   v
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (All Saints) · Mag. O ye Angels and Archangels · No Suff.
               Com. XXII Sunday after Pentecost ("Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's")

NOVEMBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Sun     ALL SAINTS                                 [1cl] w
           XXII Sunday after Pentecost (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. XXII Sunday after Pentecost ("Master")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O how glorious · No Suff.
               Com. XXII Sunday after Pentecost ("Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's")
  2  Mon     All Souls' Day                             [d]   b
           Day II within the Octave of All Saints (sd)
           Lauds   b · Ben. Let everything that hath breath · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day II within the Octave of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers b · Mag. O Lord, forsake not · No Suff.
               Com. Day II within the Octave of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
               Com. St Winifred, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
  3  Tue     Day III within the Octave of All Saints    [sd]  w
           St Winifred, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Winifred, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how glorious · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vitalis & Agricola, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
  4  Wed     Day IV within the Octave of All Saints     [sd]  w
           Ss Vitalis & Agricola, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Ss Vitalis & Agricola, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how glorious · No Suff.
               Com. St Elizabeth, Mother of St John Baptist ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  5  Thu     Day V within the Octave of All Saints      [sd]  w
           St Elizabeth, Mother of St John Baptist (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Elizabeth, Mother of St John Baptist ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how glorious · No Suff.
  6  Fri  L  Day VI within the Octave of All Saints     [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. O how glorious · No Suff.
               Com. St Willibrord, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
  7  Sat     Day VII within the Octave of All Saints    [sd]  w
           St Willibrord, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The glorious company · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Willibrord, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XXIII Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. Encompass us · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Octave of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
               Com. Four Crowned Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Octave Day of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
  8  Sun     XXIII Sunday after Pentecost               [sd]  g
           Four Crowned Martyrs (com)
           Octave Day of All Saints (gd)
           Lauds   g · Ben. For she said within herself · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Four Crowned Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
               Com. Octave Day of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. But Jesus turned Him about · No Suff.
               Com. Dedication of The Basilica of St. Saviour ("O how dreadful is this place!")
               Com. Octave Day of All Saints ("I beheld a great multitude")
               Com. St Theodore Tyro, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
  9  Mon     Dedication of The Basilica of St. Saviour  [gd]  w
           St Theodore Tyro, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Zacchaeus · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Theodore Tyro, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O how dreadful is this place! · No Suff.
               Com. Ss. Tryphon, Respicius & Nympha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
 10  Tue     Feria                                            g
           Ss. Tryphon, Respicius & Nympha, Martyrs (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. Ss. Tryphon, Respicius & Nympha, Martyrs ("For theirs is the kingdom of heaven")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Martin of Tours) · Mag. O how blessed a man is he! · No Suff.
               Com. St Mennas, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 11  Wed     St. Martin of Tours                        [gd]  w
           St Mennas, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Martin is called joyfully into Abraham's bosom · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Mennas, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O how blessed a Bishop was he! · No Suff.
               Com. St Martin I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 12  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Martin I, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Martin I, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
               Com. St Britius of Tours, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")

               Com. St Britius of Tours, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces

               Com. St Gregory Palamas, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")

           St Gregory Palamas, Bishop & Confessor (com)

               Com. St Gregory Palamas, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   Preces

           Lauds   g · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. The kingdom of heaven · Suff.
 16  Mon     Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Blessed be the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. My soul doth magnify the Lord · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory the Wonder-worker, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Hilda of Whitby, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")

           St Hilda of Whitby, Virgin (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Gregory the Wonder-worker, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
               Com. St Hilda of Whitby, Virgin ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Dedication of the Basilica of the Holy Apostles Peter & Paul) · Mag. O how dreadful is this place! · No Suff.

           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O how dreadful is this place! · No Suff.
               Com. St Pontianus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 19  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Pontianus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Pontianus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces

 20  Fri  L  St Edmund, King & Martyr                   [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. He that hateth his life · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. All generations shall call me blessed · No Suff.
               Com. St Edmund, King & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
               Com. St Columbanus, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Gelasius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 21  Sat     Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary    [gd]  w
           St Columbanus, Abbot (com)
           St Gelasius, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. O Mary · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Columbanus, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. St Gelasius, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · I fol. (XXIV & Last Sunday after Pentecost) · Mag. I have set watchmen · No Suff.
               Com. Presentation of the Blessed Virgin Mary ("O Holy Mary")
               Com. St. Cecilia ("The musicians played, and Cecilia sang unto the Lord")
 22  Sun     XXIV & Last Sunday after Pentecost         [sd]  g
           St. Cecilia (d)
           Lauds   g · Ben. When ye, therefore, shall see · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Cecilia ("The musicians played, and Cecilia sang unto the Lord")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers g · II prec. · Mag. Amen, I say unto you, this generation shall not… · No Suff.
               Com. St. Clement I, Bishop and Martyr ("Let us all")
               Com. St. Cecilia ("The musicians played, and Cecilia sang unto the Lord")
               Com. St Felicitas, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 23  Mon     St. Clement I, Bishop and Martyr           [d]   r
           St Felicitas, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   r · Ben. As he began to move toward the sea · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Felicitas, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. All the nations · No Suff.
               Com. St Chrysogonus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 24  Tue     Feria                                            g
           St Chrysogonus, Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. The Lord hath raised up · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Chrysogonus, Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St Catherine of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr) · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
 25  Wed     St Catherine of Alexandria, Virgin & Martyr [d]   r
           Lauds   r · Ben. The kingdom of heaven · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Come, thou Bride of Christ · No Suff.
               Com. St Peter of Alexandria, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 26  Thu     Feria                                            g
           St Peter of Alexandria, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   g · Ben. Let us serve the Lord · No Preces · Suff.
               Com. St Peter of Alexandria, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. Show strength with thine arm · Suff.
 27  Fri  L  Feria                                            g
           Lauds   g · Ben. Through the tender mercy of our God · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers g · Mag. He hath put down the mighty · Suff.
 28  Sat     Vigil of St. Andrew, Apostle               [s]   v
           Lauds   v · Ben. O Lord, give light · No Preces · Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (I Sunday of Advent) · Mag. Behold, the Name of the Lord · No Suff.
               Com. St Saturninus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 29  Sun  L  I Sunday of Advent                         [2cl] v
           St Saturninus, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Saturninus, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Andrew, Apostle) · Mag. One of the two · No Suff.
               Com. I Sunday of Advent ("The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee")
 30  Mon  L§ St. Andrew, Apostle                        [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Yield up to us · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. When blessed Andrew · No Suff.

DECEMBER
------------------------------------------------------------
  1  Tue  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Before they came together · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St Peter Chrysologus, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor) · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
               Com. St Bibiana, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
  2  Wed  L§ St Peter Chrysologus, Bishop, Confessor & Doctor [d]   w
           St Bibiana, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Bibiana, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. O Teacher right excellent · No Suff.
  3  Thu  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Blessed art thou · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. I will wait · No Suff.
               Com. St Barbara, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
  4  Fri  L§ Feria                                            v
           St Barbara, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. He that is both God and man · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Barbara, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Out of Egypt · No Suff.
               Com. St Sabbas of Judæa, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
  5  Sat  L  Feria                                            v
           St Sabbas of Judæa, Abbot (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Fear not · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Sabbas of Judæa, Abbot ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (II Sunday of Advent) · Mag. Come, O Lord, in peace · No Suff.
               Com. St. Nicholas ("Behold a great priest")
  6  Sun  L  II Sunday of Advent                        [2cl] v
           St. Nicholas (d)
           Lauds   v · Ben. Now when John · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St. Nicholas ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · II prec. · Mag. Art thou he that art to come · No Suff.
               Com. St. Ambrose ("O Teacher right excellent")
               Com. St. Nicholas ("Behold a great priest")
  7  Mon  L§ St. Ambrose                                [gd]  w
           Vigil of the Conception (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Vigil of the Conception ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary) · Mag. All generations · No Suff.
               Com. St. Ambrose ("O Teacher right excellent")
  8  Tue  L  Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary      [2cl] w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
  9  Wed  L§ Day II within Conception Octave            [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
               Com. St Melchiades, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 10  Thu  L  Day III within Conception Octave           [sd]  w
           St Melchiades, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Melchiades, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
               Com. St Damasus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
 11  Fri  L§ Day IV within Conception Octave            [sd]  w
           St Damasus, Bishop & Confessor (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Damasus, Bishop & Confessor ("Behold a great priest")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
 12  Sat  L  Day V within Conception Octave             [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers p · I fol. (III Sunday of Advent (Gaudete)) · Mag. Before me · No Suff.
               Com. St Lucy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Herman of Alaska, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Day VI within Conception Octave ("Thou art all fair, O Mary")
 13  Sun  L  III Sunday of Advent (Gaudete)             [2cl] p
           St Lucy, Virgin & Martyr (gd)
           St Herman of Alaska, Priest & Confessor (com)
           Day VI within Conception Octave (sd)
           Lauds   p · Ben. He shall sit upon the throne · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Lucy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
               Com. St Herman of Alaska, Priest & Confessor ("Lord, thou deliveredst unto me five talents")
               Com. Day VI within Conception Octave ("Thou art all fair, O Mary")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers p · II prec. · Mag. Blessed art thou · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within Conception Octave ("Thou art all fair, O Mary")
               Com. St Lucy, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 14  Mon  L§ Day VII within Conception Octave           [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Octave of the Conception of the B.V.M) · Mag. All generations · No Suff.
 15  Tue  L  Octave of the Conception of the B.V.M      [gd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. The Lord God said · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Let us celebrate · No Suff.
               Com. St Eusebius of Vercelli, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
 16  Wed  L§ Advent Ember Wednesday                     [f2]  v
           St Eusebius of Vercelli, Bishop & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   v · Ben. The angel Gabriel · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Eusebius of Vercelli, Bishop & Martyr ("Whosoever shall confess me")
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. Behold the handmaid · No Suff.
 17  Thu  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Let your soul watch · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O Wisdom · No Suff.
               Com. Expectation of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 18  Fri  L§ Advent Ember Friday                        [f2]  v
           Expectation of the B.V.M (gd)
           Lauds   v · Ben. For lo, as soon · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Expectation of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O Adonai · No Suff.
               Com. Expectation of the B.V.M ("Holy Mary, succour the miserable")
 19  Sat  L§ Advent Ember Saturday                      [f2]  v
           Lauds   v · Ben. How shall this be · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · I fol. (IV Sunday of Advent) · Mag. O Root of Jesse · No Suff.
 20  Sun  L  IV Sunday of Advent                        [2cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Hail, Mary, full of grace · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers r · I fol. (St. Thomas, Apostle) · Mag. Because thou hast seen me · No Suff.
               Com. IV Sunday of Advent ("Hail, Mary, full of grace")
 21  Mon  L§ St. Thomas, Apostle                        [2cl] r
           Lauds   r · Ben. Because thou hast seen me · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Because thou hast seen me · No Suff.
 22  Tue  L  Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. Awake, awake · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O King of the Gentiles · No Suff.
 23  Wed  L§ Feria                                            v
           Lauds   v · Ben. I will place salvation · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   Preces
           Vespers v · Mag. O Emmanuel, our King · No Suff.
 24  Thu  L§ VIGIL OF THE NATIVITY OF OUR LORD          [1cl] v
           Lauds   v · Ben. The Saviour · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Nativity of Our Lord Jesus Christ) · Mag. When the sun hath risen · No Suff.
               Com. St Anastasia, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
 25  Fri     NATIVITY OF OUR LORD JESUS CHRIST          [1cl] w
           St Anastasia, Virgin & Martyr (com)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Glory · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. St Anastasia, Virgin & Martyr ("This is one of the wise virgins")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. Today · No Suff.
               Com. St. Stephen, Protomartyr ("And Stephen")
 26  Sat     St. Stephen, Protomartyr                   [2cl] r
           Day II within the Nativity Octave (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. And Stephen · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day II within the Nativity Octave ("Glory")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Devout men · No Suff.
               Com. St. John, Apostle and Evangelist ("This is the same John")
 27  Sun     St. John, Apostle and Evangelist           [2cl] w
           Day III within the Nativity Octave (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. This is the same John · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day III within the Nativity Octave ("Glory")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. There went this saying abroad · No Suff.
               Com. Holy Innocents ("These are they")
 28  Mon     Holy Innocents                             [2cl] r
           Day IV within the Nativity Octave (sd)
           Lauds   r · Ben. These are they · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day IV within the Nativity Octave ("Glory")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers r · II prec. · Mag. Then were innocent children · No Suff.
               Com. Sunday within the Octave of the Nativity ("The Child Jesus")
 29  Tue     Sunday within the Octave of the Nativity   [sd]  w
           Day V within the Nativity Octave (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. While all things · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day V within the Nativity Octave ("Glory")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · II prec. · Mag. The Child Jesus · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Nativity Octave ("The King of Peace")
               Com. Day V within the Nativity Octave ("The King of Peace")
 30  Wed     Day VI within the Nativity Octave          [sd]  w
           Lauds   w · Ben. Glory · No Preces · No Suff.
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (St. Sylvester I, Bishop) · Mag. O thou Priest and Bishop · No Suff.
               Com. Day VI within the Nativity Octave ("The King of Peace")
               Com. Day VII within the Nativity Octave ("The King of Peace")
 31  Thu     St. Sylvester I, Bishop                    [d]   w
           Day VII within the Nativity Octave (sd)
           Lauds   w · Ben. Well done · No Preces · No Suff.
               Com. Day VII within the Nativity Octave ("Glory")
           Hours   No Preces
           Vespers w · I fol. (Circumcision of Our Lord) · Mag. God · No Suff.
               Com. St. Sylvester I, Bishop ("Behold a great priest")
